quranicRelevance: Enjoining covenants/ethics (Wasiyyah - 'wassa'), bequeathing estate/legacy, and advising one another (Tawasaw).

keyDerivations.meaning: To enjoin, command, advise, or bequeath

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَصَّى بِهَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ (2:132): Abraham enjoined/willed this (the legacy of monotheism).
تَوَاصَوْا بِالصَّبْرِ: Enjoined/advised one another to patience.
discardedNoise: Specific court procedures for testament execution.
summaryEn: The root denotes enjoining and legacy, covering both the moral injunctions (Wasiyah) and mutual advice (Tawasaw) to patience and truth.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الإيجاب والوصية، مغطياً العهد والوصية المالية والشرعية (وصية)، والتواصي الأخلاقي المتبادل (تواصوا).
rawText: أَوْصَى لَهُ بِالثُّلُثِ 4 He bequeathed to him the third of the property. (MA.) === أَوْصَى He made his will. === See 2. تَوَاصَوْا 6 They enjoined, charged, bade, ordered, or commanded, one another. See an ex. voce تَبَاعَثُوا. وَصِىٌّ A person commissioned; a commissioned agent: (K:) an executor appointed by a will. وَصِيَّةٌ An injunction, a charge, bidding, order, or command: (K:) an admonition, with an endeavour to persuade: and a command: its place may be supplied by any word in which is the meaning of أَمْرٌ. (Msb.) === And A will, or testament. (K, * TA.) وَصَّاهُ 2 and 🡻أَوْصَاهُ He enjoined him; charged him; bade him; ordered him: (K, &c.:) he commanded him, بِكَذَا, to do such a thing. (Msb.)
